revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Twelve Devas]][[es:Doce Devas]][[ja:十二天]][[ru:12 Божеств]][[zh:十二天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Twelve Devas]][[es:Doce Devas]][[ja:十二天]][[ru:12 Божеств]][[zh:十二天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Twelve Devas]][[es:Doce Devas]][[ja:十二天]][[ru:12 Божеств]][[zh:十二天]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Twelve Devas]][[es:Doce Devas]][[ja:十二天]][[ru:12 Божеств]][[zh:十二天]]
{{Cite_sources|date=May 20, 2022 (Friday) 09:18 (UTC)}} '''Jūniten''' (十二天) refers to twelve {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|qid=Q178885|lt=Deities|1=Deity}} known as the "Tenbu" (天部) in {{ill|Buddhism|en|Buddhism|qid=Q748|lt=Buddhism|1=Buddhism}}, who serve as protective deities (護法善神). In {{ill|Mikkyō|en|Mikkyō|qid=Q336102|lt=Esoteric Buddhism|1=Mikkyō}}, they are highly regarded alongside the {{ill|Four Heavenly Kings|en|Four Heavenly Kings|qid=Q1068367|lt=Four Heavenly Kings|1=Four Heavenly Kings}}. Among the Jūniten, deities protecting the eight directions (north, south, east, west, northeast, southeast, northwest, and southwest) are called "Happōten" (八方天) or "Gosei Happōten" (護世八方天), and when deities protecting heaven and earth are included, they are referred to as "Jūten" (十天).<ref name=kodama>{{Cite book | author = Yoshitaka Kodama | title = 印と梵字ご利益・功徳事典 | publisher = Gakken Publishing | page = 256 | year = 2009 | isbn = 9784054041875 }}</ref>
== Origins ==
In Buddhism, the "Tenbu" or celestial deities originated from ancient {{ill|Hindu mythology|en|Hindu mythology|qid=Q878062|lt=Indian mythology|1=Hindu mythology}} and {{ill|Brahmanism|ja|バラモン教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Brahmanism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Brahmanism|qid=Q889766}}, where they were incorporated into Buddhist practices as protective deities. The Jūniten includes eight deities of the directions (Happōten), along with two deities of heaven and earth and two of the sun and moon.<ref name=kodama/>
== List ==
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small; white-space:nowrap" ! Japanese Name !! Reading !! Sanskrit Name !! Reading !! {{ill|Bīja|ja|種子 (密教)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bīja|qq=}} !! Direction |- | {{ill|Śakra (Buddhism)|en|Śakra (Buddhism)|qid=Q1444745|lt=Taishakuten|1=Śakra (Buddhism)}} || Taishakuten || Indra || Indora || ī イー [[File:BonjiIi.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|East|en|East|qid=Q684|lt=East|1=East}} |- | {{ill|Katen|ja|火天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katen|1=Katen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11566862}} || Katen || Agni || Aguni || a ア [[File:BonjiA.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Southeast|ja|東南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southeast|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 南東|1=Southeast|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6452640}} |- | {{ill|Enmaten|ja|焔摩天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enmaten|1=Enmaten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567277}} || Enmaten || Yama || Yama || yaṃ エン [[File:BonjiYam.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|South|en|South|qid=Q667|lt=South|1=South}} |- | {{ill|Rakshasa|en|Rakshasa|qid=Q1049467|lt=Rasetsuten|1=Rakshasa}} || Rasetsuten || Rākṣasa / Nirṛti || Rākṣasa / Nirriti || nṛ ニリ [[File:BonjiNr.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Southwest|ja|西南|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Southwest|1=Q11627311|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627311}} |- | {{ill|Suiten|ja|水天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suiten|qq=}} || Suiten || Varuṇa || Varuna || va バ [[File:BonjiVa.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|West|en|West|qid=Q679|lt=West|1=West}} |- | {{ill|Fūten|ja|風天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fūten|1=Fūten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665458}} || Fūten || Vāyu || Vāyu || vā バー [[File:BonjiVaa.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Northwest|ja|西北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northwest|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 北西|1=Northwest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5491373}} |- | {{ill|Vaiśravaṇa|en|Vaiśravaṇa|qid=Q866315|lt=Bishamonten|1=Vaiśravaṇa}} || Bishamonten || Vaiśravaṇa || Vaishravana || vai バイ [[File:BonjiVai.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|North|en|North|qid=Q659|lt=North|1=North}} |- | {{ill|Ishana|en|Ishana|qid=Q608366|lt=Izanaten|1=Ishana}} || Izanaten || Īśāna || Ishana || i イ [[File:Bonji-I.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Northeast|ja|東北|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Northeast|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東北地方|1=Tōhoku region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q129465}} |- | {{ill|Brahmā (Buddhism)|en|Brahmā (Buddhism)|qid=Q289162|lt=Bonten|1=Brahmā (Buddhism)}} || Bonten || Brahmā || Brahma || bra ボラ [[File:BonjiBra.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Tian|en|Tian|qid=Q1364799|lt=Heaven|1=Tian}} (Above) |- | {{ill|Jiten|ja|地天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jiten|1=Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123527556}} || Jiten || Pṛthivī || Prithivi || pṛ ピリ [[File:BonjiPr.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Land|en|Land|qid=Q11081619|lt=Earth|1=Land}} (Below) |- | {{ill|Nitten|ja|日天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nitten|1=Surya in Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505234}} || Nitten || Sūrya / Āditya || Surya / Aditya || ā アー [[File:BonjiA.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|qid=Q525|lt=Sun|1=Sun}} |- | {{ill|Chandra|en|Chandra|qid=Q1061795|lt=Gatten|1=Chandra}} || Gatten || Candra || Chandra || ca シャ [[File:BonjiCa.png|50px|right]] || {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|qid=Q405|lt=Moon|1=Moon}} |}
The Four Heavenly Kings align only with Bishamonten (north), known as Tamonten among the Four Kings.
The solar deity Sūrya and the lunar deity Candra also appear among the {{ill|Navagraha|en|Navagraha|qid=Q1078632|lt=Navagraha|1=Navagraha}} celestial bodies.
== Japanese Representations ==
{{ill|Tō-ji|en|Tō-ji|qid=Q1046403|lt=Tōji|1=Tō-ji}} houses some of the earliest examples of Jūniten images from the early Heian period (9th century).
The Kyoto National Museum holds a collection of Jūniten hanging scrolls (late Heian period, Daiji 2, 1127, National Treasure), which were used during the {{ill|Goshichinichi no Mishihō|ja|後七日御修法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshichinichi no Mishihō|1=Goshichinichi Mishiho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124680869}}, an esoteric ritual performed in the imperial court.
Other notable examples include the National Treasure Jūniten images of {{ill|Saidai-ji|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidai-ji|qq=}} in Nara, featuring animal mounts and distinct depictions.
== Gallery == <gallery> File:Taishakuten.jpg|Taishakuten File:Katen.jpg|Katen File:Enmaten KNM.jpg|Enmaten File:Rasetsuten.jpg|Rasetsuten File:Suiten KNM.jpg|Suiten File:Futen.jpg|Fūten File:Bishamonten KNM.jpg|Bishamonten File:Ishanaten KNM.jpg|Izanaten File:Bonten.jpg|Bonten File:Jiten.jpg|Jiten File:Nitten KNM.jpg|Nitten File:Gatten.jpg|Gatten </gallery>
